human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for corporate-actions boundary discipline, source quality, announcement and election workflow posture, entitlement and allocation evidence handling, exception routing, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to corporate-actions execution such as announcement verification, event-date control awareness, option and election packaging, instruction acknowledgement monitoring, entitlement and allocation tracking, and completion evidence capture.
Authority sources cover FINRA ex-date and company-related action rules, DTCC distributions and reorganizations workflow references, and FINRA or SEC books-and-records control standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete artifacts, rework queues, completion-evidence refusal, realistic corporate-actions terminology, conflicting-policy handling, overgeneralization guardrails, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/capital-markets-and-asset-management/corporate-actions-specialist/ so corporate-actions work stays distinct from settlement, proxy, tax, issuer-services, legal, and portfolio-decision lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around election authority, tax or legal interpretation, issuer-side actions, overgeneralization of corporate-action types, and completion without evidence.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated corporate-actions specialist that supports evidence-backed event processing, exception routing, and audit-ready closure outputs.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
